title: Infective Endocarditis (IVDU)
chief_complaint: Fever for 2 weeks. I'm tired, achy, and lost weight. I have these red spots on my fingers and my urine looks dark.

physical_exam.Cardiac: New holosystolic murmur grade 4/6 at LLSB radiating to axilla (tricuspid regurgitation). No diastolic murmur.
physical_exam.Skin: Osler nodes on fingertips (tender), Janeway lesions on palms (non-tender), splinter hemorrhages in nail beds
physical_exam.Abdomen: Splenomegaly (3cm below costal margin). Mild RUQ tenderness.
physical_exam.Eyes: Roth spots on fundoscopy (retinal hemorrhages with pale centers). Conjunctival petechiae.
physical_exam.Extremities: Track marks in antecubital fossae (IVDU stigmata)

hidden_data.echo: Large vegetation on tricuspid valve (2.5cm). Severe tricuspid regurgitation. No annular abscess. Normal LV function.
hidden_data.cbc: Hb 8.5 (anemia of chronic disease), WBC 14,500, Platelets 160,000
hidden_data.cmp: Creatinine 1.5 (glomerulonephritis from immune complex deposition), AST 60, ALT 55

correct_diagnosis: Infective Endocarditis (Tricuspid Valve) from MSSA in IVDU with Immune Complex Glomerulonephritis
key_learning_points: Duke criteria: 2 major (persistent bacteremia + vegetation on echo) OR 1 major + 3 minor (fever, IVDU, vascular phenomena, immunologic phenomena, positive cultures)
IVDU-associated IE: Right-sided (tricuspid > pulmonary). S. aureus most common (70%). Better prognosis than left-sided IE
Vascular phenomena: Janeway lesions (non-tender, palms/soles), conjunctival hemorrhages, splinter hemorrhages. Immunologic: Osler nodes (tender, fingertips), Roth spots, GN, positive RF
Treatment: MSSA = Oxacillin/Nafcillin 2g IV q4h ×6 weeks (or Cefazolin 2g IV q8h). MRSA = Vancomycin or Daptomycin. Right-sided uncomplicated = 2-4 weeks
Surgery indications: Vegetation >20mm + emboli, persistent bacteremia >7 days, heart failure, annular abscess, prosthetic valve. Right-sided IE rarely needs surgery
